articles.title: IMD Weather Alert: Mumbai Humid Heat, Maharashtra Heatwave & Patchy Rain; Odisha 42°C - Pratidin Time
articles.full_text:
Even as June nears its end, Mumbai and several parts of Maharashtra continue to reel under persistent hot and humid weather conditions, with the monsoon yet to show any consistent progress. Skies remain largely dry across the financial capital, leaving residents struggling with uncomfortable daytime conditions.
According to the report, the India Meteorological Department (IMD), in its latest weather update, has stated that several regions of Maharashtra are likely to see a further rise in temperatures. At the same time, isolated districts may experience spells of rain and thunderstorms, indicating highly uneven weather patterns across the state.
Konkan Region to Remain Humid, Isolated Rain Likely
According to the IMD, hot and humid conditions are expected to dominate parts of the Konkan belt. This includes Thane, Mumbai, Raigad and Ratnagiri, where sultry weather is likely to persist.
However, the weather department has also indicated that some parts of the region may receive light rainfall during the afternoon hours. In addition, Sindhudurg district may witness isolated light rain activity, offering brief relief from the prevailing heat.
North Maharashtra to Witness Heatwave in Pockets
In North Maharashtra, the IMD has forecast heatwave-like conditions in isolated pockets of Dhule, Jalgaon and Nashik districts.
Despite the heat warning, the weather office has also predicted the possibility of light to moderate rain accompanied by thunderstorms during the evening hours in these regions. Ahilyanagar district may also experience light rainfall activity in certain areas.
Western Maharashtra: Mixed Conditions Across Districts
Western Maharashtra is expected to see highly varied weather conditions.
Pune city is likely to receive spells of light to moderate rain, bringing partial relief from rising temperatures. On the other hand, Solapur district is expected to remain under heatwave conditions, with temperatures likely to stay high.
Meanwhile, scattered rainfall activity is also possible across Sangli, Satara and Kolhapur districts, though it is expected to remain patchy and uneven.
Marathwada Under Heat Alert, Rain Possible in Some Areas
In the Marathwada region, the IMD has issued a yellow alert for Chhatrapati Sambhajinagar, Jalna and Beed due to prevailing high temperature conditions.
At the same time, light rainfall activity is expected in Parbhani, Hingoli, Latur and Dharashiv districts. The weather department has also warned of isolated thunderstorms with rain in the Nanded district.
Vidarbha Continues to Suffer Heatwave Conditions
The Vidarbha region remains under significant heat stress.
Heatwave conditions are expected in Akola, Amravati and Wardha districts. Additionally, a yellow heat alert has been issued for Bhandara, Gadchiroli, Gondia, Nagpur, Yavatmal and Wardha, indicating sustained high temperature risks across large parts of the region.
No weather alert has been issued for Washim district.
Odisha Sees Extreme Heat, Temperature Touches 42.7°C
Meanwhile, in neighbouring Odisha, extreme summer conditions continue to prevail.
The district of Boudh in western Odisha recorded a maximum temperature of 42.7°C on Wednesday, marking one of the highest readings in the region this season.
Due to the severe heatwave situation, state authorities have decided to extend summer vacation for schools and anganwadi centres in several districts.
School Timings and Holiday Extension in Odisha
Schools in Bolangir, Sonepur, Ganjam, Angul and Sundargarh districts will remain closed until June 20, instead of reopening on June 18 as previously scheduled.
Since June 21 falls on a Sunday, schools in these districts will now reopen on June 22, according to official confirmation.
In the Nuapada district, authorities have ordered revised school timings due to extreme heat conditions. Classes will be conducted in the morning shift only, from 6:30 am to 11:30 am, between June 18 and June 20.
Officials further noted that schools across the state had already been shut since April 27 as part of the summer vacation schedule, which was extended in view of rising temperatures across multiple districts.'

articles.title: Mumbai Weather LATEST Update: IMD Predicts Rain in Several Maharashtra Districts, Heat Persists - Asianet Newsable
articles.full_text:
Mumbai Weather LATEST Update: IMD Predicts Rain in Several Maharashtra Districts, Heat Persists
Mumbai Weather LATEST Update: Maharashtra is witnessing contrasting weather conditions, with several districts likely to receive rain while others continue to battle intense heat. The IMD has issued alerts for both rainfall and heatwave conditions
Mumbai, Konkan Region May Receive Afternoon Showers
Residents of Mumbai continue to experience hot and sticky weather despite the advancing monsoon season. According to the IMD, humid conditions are expected to persist across parts of the Konkan region, including Thane, Mumbai, Raigad and Ratnagiri.
However, relief may arrive in the form of afternoon rainfall. Light showers are also expected in Sindhudurg district, offering some respite from the prevailing heat and humidity.
Pune, Sangli, Satara and Kolhapur Set for Rainfall
Western Maharashtra is likely to witness improved weather conditions with light to moderate rainfall forecast in Pune city. Similar rain activity is expected across Sangli, Satara and Kolhapur districts.
Meanwhile, North Maharashtra could continue to face challenging weather. Isolated heatwave conditions are likely in Dhule, Jalgaon and Nashik districts, although some areas may also witness evening thunderstorms and moderate rainfall. Ahilyanagar is expected to receive light showers.
Heatwave Alerts Issued Across Marathwada and Vidarbha
The IMD has sounded a yellow heat alert for Chhatrapati Sambhajinagar, Jalna and Beed districts in the Marathwada region. At the same time, light rain is likely in Parbhani, Hingoli, Latur and Dharashiv.
In Vidarbha, heatwave conditions are expected in Akola, Amravati and Wardha. Yellow alerts have also been issued for Bhandara, Gadchiroli, Gondia, Nagpur, Yavatmal and Wardha due to high temperatures. Some districts, including Nanded and Bhandara, may additionally experience isolated rain accompanied by thunderstorms.
